name: WireGuard Component
description: [WireGuard®](https://www.wireguard.org/) is an extremely simple yet fast and modern VPN that utilizes state-of-the-art cryptography. This component uses a **custom** implementation not developed by original authors and currently available for ESP32, ESP8266 and BK72xx microcontrollers *only*.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: The local VPN address of the device. If you intend to upload firmwares through the VPN link you probably need to copy this value to the `use_address` parameter of the [Wifi](https://esphome.io/components/wifi/).

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: How often to check the connection status and the latest handshake value. Default to `10s`.

config_entries.label: Peer Endpoint
config_entries.description: The hostname of the remote peer.

config_entries.label: Peer Public Key
config_entries.description: The public key of the remote peer.

config_entries.label: Private Key
config_entries.description: The private key of the device.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Netmask
config_entries.description: The netmask for the configured address. Default to `255.255.255.255`. See section [Static routes and outgoing connections](https://esphome.io/components/wireguard#wireguard-static-routes) if outgoing connections are expected to transit through the VPN link (e.g. with [Mqtt](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt/) to a remote MQTT broker).

config_entries.label: Peer Allowed Ips
config_entries.description: A list of networks in CIDR notation (*IP/mask*) to be allowed through the tunnel. Any host (`0.0.0.0/0`  ) will be allowed if this parameter is omitted. The device own `address/32` is always added by default to this list. See section [Static routes and outgoing connections](https://esphome.io/components/wireguard#wireguard-static-routes) if outgoing connections are expected to transit through the VPN link (e.g. with [Mqtt](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt/) to a remote MQTT broker).

config_entries.label: Peer Persistent Keepalive
config_entries.description: The amount of time after which a *keepalive* packet is sent through the tunnel. By default this feature is disabled (`0s`  ). If there are NATs or firewalls between the device and the remote peer set this value to something like `25s`.

config_entries.label: Peer Port
config_entries.description: The port where remote peer is listening on. The WireGuard® default is `51820`.

config_entries.label: Peer Preshared Key
config_entries.description: The chosen pre-shared key between local device and remote peer.

config_entries.label: Reboot Timeout
config_entries.description: The amount of time to wait before rebooting the device when the remote peer is unreachable. Can be disabled by setting this to `0s`. Default to `15min`.

config_entries.label: Require Connection To Proceed
config_entries.description: Set to `true` to wait for the remote peer to be up before continuing to boot the device. Default to `false`. This can be used to delay the initialization of components that use the VPN tunnel as long as the VPN isn't ready. For example if you are using [Mqtt](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt/) to reach a remote broker you may experience boot freeze just after the setup of MQTT because it waits for the broker to be reachable, but the connection cannot be established until the VPN link is active too. To bypass such deadlock set this parameter to `true` in order to not initialize MQTT until the remote peer is up.
